title: LIKELY() seems to cause unexpected affinity conversion for rowid
description:
mrigger added on 2019-08-22 14:34:30:
Consider the following test case:
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES ('a');
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE LIKELY(t0.rowid) <= '0'; -- unexpected: row is not fetched
Unexpectedly, no row is fetched. The expression LIKELY(t0.rowid) <= '0' should evaluate to TRUE:
SELECT LIKELY(t0.rowid) <= '0' FROM t0; -- 1
key_bug_features: LIKELY() causes unexpected row fetching behavior with rowid
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL);
CREATE TABLE t2 (id INTEGER, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY(id) REFERENCES t1(id));
INSERT INTO t2(id, description) VALUES (1, 'related to test');
